title: The Single Eye
scripture_text: Forgetting what lies behind and straining forward to what lies ahead.
reflection: You are not the final judge of what is meaningful. The work is not measured by its outcome but by the love you bring to it. (The second hearing of this day.)
body:
The meeting that should have been an email. The deck you spent three days on that no one will read. The hour you spent on hold. The email you answered ten times.

None of this feels like a vocation. Most work does not, most of the time. That is normal. It does not mean the work is not sacred.

Every honest job, even the boring parts, is a way of saying: I will be faithful. I will show up. I will do the small things well. The Lord does not require your work to be famous. He requires it to be done.

Do the next small thing. Then the next one. The meaning comes later, or it does not, and the meaning is not the point.

The mind is a wild animal. It has not been tamed. It wanders. It loops. It invents conversations that will never happen. It rehearses grievances from 2007. It makes shopping lists. It composes apologies to people who have died.

The mind, left alone, is exhausting. And you, today, are not alone with it. The Lord of the universe, who holds every thought in the palm of His hand, is in the room with the mind. He is not surprised by it. He made it. He can hold it.

You do not have to master the mind. You only have to bring it back. To the breath. To the word. To the chair. To the now. The Lord meets you in the bringing-back, every time.

One thing, at a time. The next small thing. The Lord meets you in the small, focused moments.

The mind is a wild animal. It has not been tamed. It wanders. It loops. It invents conversations that will never happen. It rehearses grievances from 2007. It makes shopping lists. It composes apologies to people who have died.

The mind, left alone, is exhausting. And you, today, are not alone with it. The Lord of the universe, who holds every thought in the palm of His hand, is in the room with the mind. He is not surprised by it. He made it. He can hold it.

You do not have to master the mind. You only have to bring it back. To the breath. To the word. To the chair. To the now. The Lord meets you in the bringing-back, every time.

There is a way of being in the world that does not require you to be ahead, or impressive, or on top of it. The world will not validate this way. The world will tell you to hurry. The Lord is telling you to be still. The Lord is telling you to be here. The Lord is the one who sees you, right now, in this chair, with this breath, with this much strength, with this much doubt. He sees you. He is with you. The seeing is the practice. The being-with is the gift.

One thing, at a time. The next small thing. The Lord meets you in the small, focused moments.

And this is the second time around. You are not new to this. You are old to this. The Lord knows you. He has seen this day before, in you, and He is not surprised. He is the same. He is the same today.
prayer: Lord, give me patience with this work. Help me to do it as unto You, even when I cannot see You. Amen.
